stockMarket.summary: Global markets are under pressure as geopolitical tensions escalate. President Trump's remarks regarding a potential 2-3 week continuation of the Iran conflict and 'extremely hard' strikes have rattled investor sentiment. Major U.S. indices are reacting to this hawkish turn, with the Nasdaq 100 officially entering correction territory (10% down from peak).
notableMoves: Oil surge: WTI rose ~8.4% to $107.48, Brent jumped ~8% to $111.80.
Market sentiment: Nasdaq 100 correction (-10%). U.S. stock futures down.
Precious metals: Gold fell 3.4% to $4,648/oz, Silver down 6.2% to $71.39.

exhibitions: Matisse's Jazz: Rhythms in Color (Art Institute of Chicago, through June 1)
More is More is More: A Melody of Maximalism (Art Center Waco, through April 25)
Picking Up the Piecework: A Legacy of Creativity Through the Lens of Mary Hartten (Grand Center for Arts and Culture, through April 24)
trends: Renewed collector interest in figurative art (e.g., David Hockney, Andrew Cooper).
Upcoming local theater highlights: 'Grumpy Old Men' musical (Minneapolis), 'Ada Lovelace: Bride of Science' (St. Paul).
